title: _prefetch_memory recall is brittle — single first-keyword query, no phrase/multi-term retrieval
what_failed: On recall questions, _prefetch_memory extracts all 4+ char non-stopwords, then queries L3/L2 with ONLY keywords[0] (the first surviving word), naively de-pluralized. 'what's the status of the supabase migration?' queries 'supabase' (or 'status' if it survives the stoplist) and ignores 'migration'. Multi-concept questions retrieve on one concept; phrase matches are impossible; word order / first-word bias dominates.
where_failed: pi_agent.py:686-697 (words = findall; keywords = filter; query = keywords[0]; single memory_read)
why_likely: Built incrementally to stop spurious searches (T-027 added a big stoplist). The fix for over-firing (be conservative, one keyword) created under-retrieval (miss everything but the first word). The pendulum swung past the target.
migration_plan: 1. Query with the top-K keywords (K=2-3), not just keywords[0]. Merge + dedup hits by id.
2. Prefer memory_search_semantic (already exists, T-097) for prefetch — embeddings handle phrasing/synonyms far better than a single BM25 keyword. Fall back to keyword read if embeddings unavailable.
3. Rank merged hits by score and keep the top 4 (current limit), so the prompt budget is unchanged.
4. Keep the is_recall gate (it works) — only change WHAT gets queried once we've decided to query.
5. Add a test: 'what's the status of the supabase migration' must retrieve a memory tagged 'supabase migration', not just 'supabase'.
implementation_notes: memory_search_semantic already exists and is exactly the right tool here — prefetch predates it and was never migrated. This is mostly a wiring change.
analysis: PERSPECTIVE: You built semantic search (T-097) and then left your most latency-sensitive recall path on a single-keyword BM25 lookup. That's the inverted-effort pattern again in miniature. The fix is to point prefetch at the better retriever you already shipped. Cheap change, large recall-quality win.
